raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Total spend for November 2024 was $627,900 against a budget of $550,000, producing an unfavourable variance of $77,900 (+14.2%). The overage is concentrated across salary lines and contractor spend, with six lines crossing the significance threshold. Three distinct drivers are present: an unbudgeted early-start Staff Engineer whose first full payroll fell in November; temporary or transitional headcount additions across Product, Operations, and G&A that were approved outside the annual budget cycle; a marketing bonus mis-posted to salaries that is expected to be reclassified; and a planned platform-migration contractor engagement (TechForce Ltd and NovaBuild Inc) that was extended mid-month, pushing Contractor Spend to nearly double budget. Notably, a contractor cost for one individual (Clara) was deliberately posted to G&A Salaries rather than Contractor Spend per CFO direction, which affects comparability between those two lines. No permanent structural headcount risk has been identified beyond the Staff Engineer hire, though formal confirmation of temporary-headcount release dates and the marketing reclassification should be obtained before the December close.

LINE COMMENTARY

Engineering Salaries | +$18,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $120,000 | Actual: $138,000
The overage is driven by the addition of Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5, $185K base) whose start date was brought forward to 28 October at her request, one week ahead of the planned 4 November start. Her first full payroll run therefore fell entirely within November, adding a cost that was not reflected in the monthly budget. Slack and the offer-letter email are consistent on the cause; the earlier start created an extra pay-period overlap that accounts for the incremental cost. No other headcount changes in Engineering have been identified. December is expected to normalise once the overlap effect drops out, though this should be confirmed against the December payroll run.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-20T12:15:00Z - slack-engineering_salaries-broad - hr-slack-2024-11-01: Priya Sharma (Staff Engineer, L5) started 28 October — first full salary run hits November. Comp: $185K base. Previous plan assumed November start but she onboarded a week early, creating one extra pay period overlap.
- Gmail - 2024-11-20T18:15:00Z - gmail-engineering_salaries-broad - gmail-2024-11-01-priya-offer: Offer letter to Priya Sharma — start date 28 October per her request. Original plan was 4 November. First full payroll: November.

Product Salaries | +$12,000 (+15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $92,000
No formal headcount approval email was found for Product Salaries. A Slack message from HR on 4 November notes that the Product salary overlap is temporary and that December is expected to return to plan, but does not name the individual or quantify the overlap cost. The $12,000 overage is consistent with a short-term headcount timing issue similar to Engineering, though without a supporting offer letter or payroll record the driver cannot be fully confirmed. A review of the Product payroll detail for November is recommended to reconcile the specific cost.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:45:00Z - slack-product_salaries-narrow - hr-slack-2024-11-04: Product overlap is temporary. December will return to plan. No other headcount changes in Product.

Marketing Salaries | +$8,400 (+14.0%) | Budget: $60,000 | Actual: $68,400
The overage appears to be the result of a payroll coding error: a bonus payment was posted to Marketing Salaries rather than the Bonus & Incentives line. Payroll confirmed the mis-posting on 15 November, and a reclassification to the correct line is expected to be processed in December. If the reclassification is completed, Marketing Salaries should revert to or near budget in December. The quantum of the bonus has not been confirmed in the available evidence, so it is not possible to assert that the $8,400 is entirely attributable to this single mis-posting without a payroll journal review.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-18T12:45:00Z - slack-marketing_salaries-narrow - hr-slack-2024-11-15: Marketing bonus posting confirmed by payroll. Coding error — should be Bonus & Incentives line. Finance team to correct in December re-class.

Operations Salaries | +$7,000 (+17.5%) | Budget: $40,000 | Actual: $47,000
The variance is attributed to temporary headcount approved during the October Operations review. Slack confirms the positions carry no permanent headcount impact and are expected to be released by 1 January 2025. No formal approval email was found in Gmail, which limits the ability to confirm the individual roles, associated cost rates, or the specific release date from documentation alone. The October ops-review approval record should be obtained to confirm the release timeline and budget impact through December.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:45:00Z - slack-operations_salaries-narrow - ops-slack-2024-11-01: Temporary ops headcount approved in October ops review. Will be released by Jan 1. No permanent headcount impact.

G&A Salaries | +$4,000 (+13.3%) | Budget: $30,000 | Actual: $34,000
The overage reflects a contractor cost for an individual named Clara that was deliberately posted to G&A Salaries rather than Contractor Spend, per CFO approval for the current quarter. This is a classification decision rather than an underlying cost overrun, but it reduces comparability between the G&A Salaries and Contractor Spend lines. Readers should note that the reported Contractor Spend actual of $38,500 does not include Clara's cost; the combined contractor-related spend is therefore higher than the Contractor Spend line alone indicates. No Gmail confirmation of the CFO approval was returned; obtaining the approval record is advisable for audit purposes.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-12T12:45:00Z - slack-g&a_salaries-narrow - finance-slack-2024-11-01: G&A salary overage is Clara's contractor cost. Should have been Contractor Spend line — CFO approved posting to G&A this quarter.

Contractor Spend | +$18,500 (+92.5%) | Budget: $20,000 | Actual: $38,500
The overage is driven by a planned platform-migration engagement involving two senior contractors — TechForce Ltd (DevOps, $750/day) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend, $700/day) — on an 8-week SOW commencing 28 October, co-signed by the CTO and CFO. A mid-month amendment extended an AWS migration specialist for two additional weeks at $6,500, also CTO-approved. CRM records the project (PLAT-001) as in progress against a $50,000 total approved budget, with Milestone 2 of 4 complete and a January go-live target. The November actual of $18,500 is recorded in CRM against that total budget. Slack estimated November contractor cost at approximately $12,000 for the two original contractors; the gap between that estimate and the $18,500 actual is likely attributable to the $6,500 extension, though the arithmetic is indicative rather than precisely reconciled. Remaining exposure against the $50,000 approved budget is approximately $31,500 and may extend into December and January depending on milestone progress. This line also excludes Clara's cost, which was posted to G&A Salaries (see above).
Sources
- Gmail - 2024-11-16T18:15:00Z - gmail-contractor_spend-broad - gmail-2024-10-25-contractor-sow: SOW executed with TechForce Ltd (DevOps contractor) and NovaBuild Inc (Backend contractor). 8-week engagement from 28 Oct. Day rate schedule attached. CTO and CFO co-signed.
- Gmail - 2024-11-16T18:45:00Z - gmail-contractor_spend-narrow - gmail-2024-11-15-contractor-extension: CTO email — AWS migration specialist engaged for 2 additional weeks. $6,500 total. Approved. Added to existing SOW as Amendment 1.
- Slack - 2024-11-16T12:15:00Z - slack-contractor_spend-broad - eng-slack-2024-10-28: Platform migration kicked off — two senior contractors (DevOps + Backend) engaged for 8 weeks from 28 Oct. Day rates $750 and $700 per day respectively. Estimated Nov cost ~$12,000. CTO approved.
- CRM - 2024-11-16T26:15:00Z - crm-contractor_spend-broad - crm-project-PLAT-001: Platform Migration Project. Status: In Progress. Budget: $50,000 (total). Contractor spend Nov: $18,500 of $50K approved budget. Milestone 2 of 4 complete. On track for January go-live.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines were reviewed and assessed as below the significance threshold. No detailed investigation was conducted.
- Sales Salaries: favourable direction variance of +$6,000 (+4.0%) against budget. Within tolerance; no further action required at this time.
- Benefits & Payroll Taxes: variance of +$4,000 (+8.0%) against budget. Likely correlated with the salary line overages noted above, as payroll taxes move in proportion to gross payroll. No independent investigation conducted.
